key_insight: A mode-resolved phase-field fatigue model separates fibre-dominated and matrix/inter-fibre damage, letting the active fracture mechanism evolve independently instead of collapsing everything into one damage variable.
engineering_relevance: This gives composite designers a more mechanistic way to predict crack mode, stiffness loss, and fatigue response across ply orientations and hole/notch geometries.
method: Puck-informed formulation with separate fatigue histories, thresholds, and resistance-degradation laws for two channels.
Implemented in Abaqus/Standard via a compact UMAT-UEL setup: one orthotropic mechanical routine plus two scalar phase-field layers.
Validated on one-element tests, parameter sweeps, and centered-notch/open-hole tension at 0°, 45°, and 90° under monotonic and cyclic loading.
evidence_signal: Using one fixed IM7/8552 material and fatigue card, the model reproduces the expected crack modes and life ordering, with 45°/90° cases failing in about 1,000 cycles and 0° cases persisting to 200,000 cycles without fibre cracking.
watch_item: The study is a numerical verification of cross-geometry consistency, so it does not yet claim calibrated experimental life prediction.
paper_title: A Puck-informed mode-resolved phase-field fatigue framework for unidirectional composites
